name: api-and-interface-design

name: debugging-and-error-recovery
description_raw: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.
description_normalized: Provides a structured triage process for root-cause debugging when tests fail, builds break, or runtime behavior is unexpected.

frontmatter_yaml.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
frontmatter_yaml.description: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, behavior doesn't match expectations, or you encounter any unexpected error. Use when you need a systematic approach to finding and fixing the root cause rather than guessing.

name: frontend-ui-engineering
description_raw: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.

frontmatter_yaml.name: frontend-ui-engineering
frontmatter_yaml.description: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces. Use when creating components, implementing layouts, managing state, or when the output needs to look and feel production-quality rather than AI-generated.

name: git-workflow-and-versioning
description_raw: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.
description_normalized: Enforces disciplined git workflow (trunk-based, atomic commits, safe branching) as the safety net for AI-accelerated development.

frontmatter_yaml.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change. Use when committing, branching, resolving conflicts, or when you need to organize work across multiple parallel streams.

name: incremental-implementation
description_raw: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.
description_normalized: Implements features in thin vertical slices — build one piece, test it, verify it, then expand — never writing large batches before testing.

frontmatter_yaml.name: incremental-implementation
frontmatter_yaml.description: Delivers changes incrementally. Use when implementing any feature or change that touches more than one file. Use when you're about to write a large amount of code at once, or when a task feels too big to land in one step.

name: performance-optimization
description_raw: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.
description_normalized: Measures then fixes performance bottlenecks — Core Web Vitals, load times, SLAs — never optimizing before profiling proves a problem exists.

frontmatter_yaml.name: performance-optimization
frontmatter_yaml.description: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist, when you suspect performance regressions, or when Core Web Vitals or load times need improvement. Use when profiling reveals bottlenecks that need fixing.

name: security-and-hardening

description_normalized: Applies security-first practices to any feature touching user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations — treating all external input as hostile.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, data storage, or external integrations. Use when building any feature that accepts untrusted data, manages user sessions, or interacts with third-party services.

description_normalized: Grounds every framework-specific implementation decision in official documentation with source citations, preventing stale training-data patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: source-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when you want authoritative, source-cited code free from outdated patterns. Use when building with any framework or library where correctness matters.

name: baku
description_raw: Sync and manage Baku app zips from claude.ai (claude-ship exports) to ~/Downloads/baku-2.0/. Use when David says 'sync baku', 'baku sync', 'baku status', 'update baku-2.0', 'new zip downloaded', 'identify baku app', or asks about downloaded project zips. Keeps baku-2.0/ current with the latest snapshot of each app, identified by component fingerprints in signatures.json.
description_normalized: Sync and manage Baku app zips from claude.ai (claude-ship exports) to ~/Downloads/baku-2.0/.

name: nano-banana
description_raw: Generate images using Nano Banana 2 (kie.ai / Gemini 3.1 Flash Image). Context assembler + image API caller. Use when user says "nano banana", "generate an image", "create an image", "/nano-banana", "make an image of", "visualise this", or wants to turn a concept, file, or session activity into an image. Supports --concept, --file, --direction, --style, --ratio, --resolution flags.
description_normalized: Generate images using Nano Banana 2 (kie.ai / Gemini 3.1 Flash Image).

name: system-audit
description_raw: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report. Unlike delivery-review (which is diff-scoped and story-focused), system-audit reads the full codebase and produces a severity-ranked health map. Use when user says: "system audit", "full audit", "codebase health check", "audit the whole system", "audit this codebase", "run a health check", "full system review", "review the whole codebase", "audit before onboarding", "what's wrong with this codebase". Supported stacks: Next.js App Router, RVETS (AppyStack), Baku (claude.ai/claude-ship), Astro, Rails, Ruby gem, AppySentinel. Accepts a manual layer manifest for any other stack.
description_normalized: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report.

name: design-html
description_raw:
Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.
Works with approved mockups from /design-shotgun, CEO plans from /plan-ceo-review,
design review context from /plan-design-review, or from scratch with a user
description. Text actually reflows, heights are computed, layouts are dynamic.
30KB overhead, zero deps. Smart API routing: picks the right Pretext patterns
for each design type. Use when: "finalize this design", "turn this into HTML",
"build me a page", "implement this design", or after any planning skill.
Proactively suggest when user has approved a design or has a plan ready. (gstack)
description_normalized: Design finalization: generates production-quality Pretext-native HTML/CSS.

collect structured feedback, and iterate. Standalone design exploration you can
run anytime. Use when: "explore designs", "show me options", "design variants",
"visual brainstorm", or "I don't like how this looks".
Proactively suggest when the user describes a UI feature but hasn't seen
what it could look like. (gstack)

name: security-audit
description_raw: Comprehensive security scanning and vulnerability detection. Includes input validation, path traversal prevention, CVE detection, and secure coding pattern enforcement. Use when: authentication implementation, authorization logic, payment processing, user data handling, API endpoint creation, file upload handling, database queries, external API integration. Skip when: read-only operations on publi
description_normalized: Comprehensive security scanning and vulnerability detection.

name: sparc-methodology
description_raw: SPARC development workflow: Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, Completion. A structured approach for complex implementations that ensures thorough planning before coding. Use when: new feature implementation, complex implementations, architectural changes, system redesign, integration work, unclear requirements. Skip when: simple bug fixes, documentation updates, configuration ch
description_normalized: SPARC development workflow: Specification, Pseudocode, Architecture, Refinement, Completion.

name: swarm-orchestration
description_raw: Multi-agent swarm coordination for complex tasks. Uses hierarchical topology with specialized agents to break down and execute complex work across multiple files and modules. Use when: 3+ files need changes, new feature implementation, cross-module refactoring, API changes with tests, security-related changes, performance optimization across codebase, database schema changes. Skip when: single fil
description_normalized: Multi-agent swarm coordination for complex tasks.

name: "V3 CLI Modernization"
description_raw: "CLI modernization and hooks system enhancement for claude-flow v3. Implements interactive prompts, command decomposition, enhanced hooks integration, and intelligent workflow automation."
description_normalized: "CLI modernization and hooks system enhancement for claude-flow v3.

name: "V3 Core Implementation"
description_raw: "Core module implementation for claude-flow v3. Implements DDD domains, clean architecture patterns, dependency injection, and modular TypeScript codebase with comprehensive testing."
description_normalized: "Core module implementation for claude-flow v3.

name: "V3 DDD Architecture"
description_raw: "Domain-Driven Design architecture for claude-flow v3. Implements modular, bounded context architecture with clean separation of concerns and microkernel pattern."
description_normalized: "Domain-Driven Design architecture for claude-flow v3.

name: "V3 Deep Integration"
description_raw: "Deep agentic-flow@alpha integration implementing ADR-001. Eliminates 10,000+ duplicate lines by building claude-flow as specialized extension rather than parallel implementation."
description_normalized: "Deep agentic-flow@alpha integration implementing ADR-001.

name: "V3 MCP Optimization"
description_raw: "MCP server optimization and transport layer enhancement for claude-flow v3. Implements connection pooling, load balancing, tool registry optimization, and performance monitoring for sub-100ms response times."
description_normalized: "MCP server optimization and transport layer enhancement for claude-flow v3.

name: "V3 Memory Unification"
description_raw: "Unify 6+ memory systems into AgentDB with HNSW indexing for 150x-12,500x search improvements. Implements ADR-006 (Unified Memory Service) and ADR-009 (Hybrid Memory Backend)."
description_normalized: "Unify 6+ memory systems into AgentDB with HNSW indexing for 150x-12,500x search improvements.

name: "V3 Security Overhaul"
description_raw: "Complete security architecture overhaul for claude-flow v3. Addresses critical CVEs (CVE-1, CVE-2, CVE-3) and implements secure-by-default patterns. Use for security-first v3 implementation."
description_normalized: "Complete security architecture overhaul for claude-flow v3.

name: "V3 Swarm Coordination"
description_raw: "15-agent hierarchical mesh coordination for v3 implementation. Orchestrates parallel execution across security, core, and integration domains following 10 ADRs with 14-week timeline."
description_normalized: "15-agent hierarchical mesh coordination for v3 implementation.

name: coder
description_raw: Implementation specialist for writing clean, efficient code following project patterns
description_normalized: Implementation specialist for writing clean, efficient code following project patterns.

name: observability-engineer
description_raw: Implements structured logging, distributed tracing, and metrics collection to correlate agent swarm activity with application telemetry
description_normalized: Implements structured logging, distributed tracing, and metrics collection to correlate agent swarm activity with application telemetry.

name: architect
description_raw: System architect for designing implementation approaches, API contracts, and module boundaries
description_normalized: System architect for designing implementation approaches, API contracts, and module boundaries.

name: implement
description_raw: Execute the implementation plan by processing and executing all tasks defined in tasks.md
description_normalized: Drives code implementation by sequentially executing each task from tasks.md.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute the implementation plan by processing and executing all tasks defined in tasks.md

name: lean.implement
description_raw: Execute the implementation plan by processing all tasks in tasks.md.
description_normalized: Lean preset override of implement — stripped variant that processes tasks.md without optional extension hooks.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Execute the implementation plan by processing all tasks in tasks.md.

name: dispatching-parallel-agents
description_raw: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies. Delegates each independent problem domain to a focused subagent with precisely crafted context, runs them concurrently, then reviews and integrates the results.
description_normalized: Parallel subagent dispatch skill for independent tasks that can run concurrently without shared state.

frontmatter_yaml.name: dispatching-parallel-agents
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when facing 2+ independent tasks that can be worked on without shared state or sequential dependencies

name: executing-plans
description_raw: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints. Loads plan, reviews critically, executes all tasks following each step exactly, stops and asks on blockers, then hands off to finishing-a-development-branch.
description_normalized: Sequential plan execution skill for running a written implementation plan task-by-task with blocking checkpoints.

frontmatter_yaml.name: executing-plans
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when you have a written implementation plan to execute in a separate session with review checkpoints

name: subagent-driven-development
description_raw: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session. Dispatches fresh implementer subagent per task (receives full task text, never reads plan file), followed by two-stage review: spec compliance reviewer then code quality reviewer. Continuous execution — no check-ins between tasks. Ends with final code review and finishing-a-development-branch.
description_normalized: Continuous plan execution skill using fresh subagents per task with mandatory two-stage spec-then-quality review gates.

frontmatter_yaml.name: subagent-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when executing implementation plans with independent tasks in the current session

name: systematic-debugging
description_raw: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes. Iron Law: NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST. Four phases: root cause investigation (gather evidence), pattern analysis (find working examples), hypothesis and testing (one variable at a time), implementation (failing test first, then fix). After 3+ failed fixes, question the architecture.
description_normalized: Systematic root-cause debugging skill that enforces evidence-first investigation through four mandatory phases before any fix.

frontmatter_yaml.name: systematic-debugging
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when encountering any bug, test failure, or unexpected behavior, before proposing fixes

name: test-driven-development
description_raw: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code. Iron Law: NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST. Red-Green-Refactor cycle: write failing test, verify it fails for the right reason, write minimal code to pass, verify passes, refactor. No exceptions — delete code written before tests and start over. Includes Rationalizations table of verbatim agent excuses.
description_normalized: Strict TDD skill enforcing test-first development with Iron Law, Red-Green-Refactor cycle, and pre-emptive rationalization counters.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code

name: using-git-worktrees
description_raw: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback. Detects existing isolation first, defers to native harness tools, falls back to manual git worktree with safety verification, then runs project setup and clean baseline test.
description_normalized: Workspace isolation skill that detects existing worktrees, defers to native harness tools, and falls back to git worktrees with safety checks.

frontmatter_yaml.name: using-git-worktrees
frontmatter_yaml.description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - ensures an isolated workspace exists via native tools or git worktree fallback
